title: Ceph Placement Group Degraded or Peering Stuck States
dataSources: `ceph pg dump --format json` or `ceph pg stat` emitted on a timer into Splunk as `sourcetype=ceph:pg`
app: Scripted input or cron + `curl`/`ceph` wrapper posting JSON to HEC
spl:
index=storage sourcetype="ceph:pg" earliest=-30m
| where NOT match(pg_state, "(?i)active\+clean")
| stats dc(pg_id) as affected_pgs values(pg_state) as states latest(pool_name) as pool by cluster_name
| where affected_pgs > 0
| sort - affected_pgs
description: PGs that stay in `peering`, `degraded`, `undersized`, or `stuck` states mean data is not fully redundant; prolonged states risk data loss if additional OSDs fail.
value: Narrows blast radius to specific pools and PG counts so on-call engineers can target OSD hosts, CRUSH rules, or network partitions before the cluster blocks I/O.
implementation: Run a privileged script every 5 minutes on a management host with keyring access: parse `ceph pg dump_stuck unclean` or full JSON, flatten `pgid`, `state`, `pool_name`, and `cluster_name`, and send via HEC. Complement UC-6.1.14 (`ceph:status`)—this UC focuses on PG-level persistence, not the summary health string.
visualization: Single value (count of bad PGs), treemap (by pool), table (pg_state, count).

references.title: Splunk Docs — Send data to HTTP Event Collector

knownFalsePositives: Short spikes during approved changes, maintenance windows, or known batch jobs can match the rule; confirm against the vendor console and change calendar.
grandmaExplanation: We help you see how your arrays and related gear are doing before small issues turn into full outages or restore surprises.
